item.title: workspace/memory/ and tier directories created without mode 0700 — conversation history world-listable on multi-user systems
item.intent: workspace/memory/ and all tier subdirectories (hot/warm/cold) must be created with mode 0700 on all code paths so no other user can list or access conversation history
item.description: Two code paths create workspace/memory and its tier subdirectories (hot/warm/cold) without mode 0700. (1) scaffold.ts SCAFFOLD_DIRS array (line 32) lists 'workspace/memory' — it is created in the standard loop at line 68 via mkdirSync without mode, so the directory is created at 0755. Only 'security' and 'cloud' are in SCAFFOLD_DIRS_SECRET. (2) src/evolve/memory/lifecycle.ts line 73: mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) in saveManifest() creates workspace/memory without mode. (3) src/evolve/memory/lifecycle.ts line 85: mkdirSync(join(deployDir, MEMORY_BASE, tier), { recursive: true }) in ensureTierDirs() creates hot/warm/cold without mode. workspace/memory contains conversation history, PII-masked archives, and summarized agent decisions — the most sensitive non-credential data in the deployment. Any user on a multi-user system can ls workspace/memory/ to enumerate files.
acceptanceCriteria: workspace/memory/ is created with mode 0700 — no other local user can list or enumerate conversation history files
workspace/memory/hot, workspace/memory/warm, and workspace/memory/cold are each created with mode 0700
File permissions on existing files within workspace/memory/ are not altered by this fix
All tests pass with no regressions
